taskDescription: Click on the key respiratory physiology terms in the text below.
textField:
The *total* *lung* *capacity* is the sum of *vital* capacity and *residual* volume. The *functional* *residual* capacity is the volume remaining after a normal tidal expiration. *Spirometry* can measure all lung volumes except *residual* volume, which requires *helium* dilution or body *plethysmography*.

Oxygen is transported mainly bound to *haemoglobin* in red blood cells. The *oxygen-haemoglobin* *dissociation* curve is *sigmoid* in shape. A rightward shift (the *Bohr* effect) occurs with increased *temperature*, increased *PCO2*, decreased *pH*, and increased *2,3-DPG*, facilitating oxygen unloading in active tissues. *Fetal* haemoglobin has higher oxygen affinity due to reduced binding of 2,3-DPG.

The *medullary* respiratory centre controls the basic rhythm of breathing. *Peripheral* *chemoreceptors* in the *carotid* body detect low *PaO2*, while *central* chemoreceptors respond to *hydrogen* ions in the CSF. *Carbon* *dioxide* is transported as *bicarbonate* (70%), *carbaminohaemoglobin* (23%), and dissolved in plasma (7%). The enzyme *carbonic* *anhydrase* catalyses the hydration of CO2.

overallFeedback.feedback: Good effort! Revisit the terms you missed.
Excellent! You identified the key respiratory terms accurately.
